actual.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

deltas.product_description: Webinars exploring hardware support for artificial intelligence

I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

actual.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.

deltas.product_description: Professional development and learning strategies

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

deltas.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

actual.review_text: Azure Virtual Network Manager (AvNM) allows you to manage your networks at scale, but it's not just for the big companies. Anyone can benefit from the features provided. actual.review_text: Excited to see Virtual Network Verifier (or VNV as we call it) get launched to public preview!! One of the biggest challenges for customers is being able to verify traffic flows within their complex configurations. With VNV customers can now very quickly answer questions like "Why can't my VM reach my database?" Please take a moment to go try it for yourself.

deltas.product_description: Technology product launch and customer engagement

prediction.review_text:
Excited to share some insights on the evolving routing management capabilities in cloud environments! 🌐 As we continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with Azure, the importance of efficient and secure routing cannot be overstated. 

In today's cloud-first world, managing routing effectively is crucial for ensuring seamless connectivity and performance. With the recent advancements in Azure, we are now able to leverage features like User Defined Routes (UDR) and Network Security Groups (NSG) to enhance our routing strategies. These tools not only simplify the management of network traffic but also bolster our security posture, allowing us to address customer pain points more effectively.

I encourage my fellow engineers and tech enthusiasts to dive into the documentation and explore how these capabilities can be integrated into your projects. Let's continue to share our experiences and learn from one another as we navigate this exciting landscape together! 

A big shout-out to my team for their hard work in making these enhancements a reality. Your dedication to operational simplicity and security-first design truly makes a difference! 💪 #Azure #CloudNetworking #RoutingManagement #Teamwork

actual.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

deltas.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

actual.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

Working with Indian developers feels different. There's this hunger to solve problems no matter how complex they get.

But most international founders miss this:

It's not about finding ANY Indian developer. It's about finding the RIGHT technical implementation partner who bridges your vision with flawless execution.

That's exactly what CodingRippler is built for.

We're the technical implementation partner for this era. Matching world-class Indian talent to your specific business needs. Whether it's n8n automation, API integrations, or complex system builds.

Ready to discuss your technical needs? DM me - I personally handle every partnership See our client results:[

#TechnicalPartnership #StartupFounders #n8nAutomation #APIIntegration #TechResources #IndianDevelopers #TechnicalTeam #AutomationExperts #BacklogClearing #TechImplementation #ScaleUp #TechPartner #QualityDelivery #CodingRippler

actual.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

deltas.product_description: Webinars exploring hardware support for artificial intelligence

actual.review_text:
I just got off a call with a startup founder who asked something that made me write this post:

"Do you guys work 24/7? I sent that bug report at 1 PM CST and it was fixed before my 4 PM meeting. That's midnight in India!"

We don't work 24/7. But our young technical minds treat every project as a chance to learn and grow. Your business challenges become their expertise.

When it's your lunch break, it's prime time in India. And when something's broken for your business, they take personal ownership to get it fixed.

This is where Indian technical talent truly shines:

→ When requirements change mid-project, they adapt without complaints
→ They iterate until it works perfectly, not just "good enough" 
→ Problem-solving that goes beyond the immediate fix 
→ They genuinely care about your business success, not just completion

actual.review_text:
A new IndabaXEgypt webinar coming next week 
 
When: Saturday 27th March, 5pm Cairo time
Title: "Hardware Support for AI and Vice Versa"
Speaker: Prof.Mohamed Zahran
Registration link: 

This talk is unique as it will be the first IndabaXEgypt webinar around hardware support for AI applications and we are we lucky to have Prof. Mohamed Zahran to explore this space with us. Mohamed Zahran is a professor with the Computer Science Department, Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences at New York University (NYU).

About the Talk:
As AI, especially ML, became a buzzword about a decade ago, a lot of research, both in industry and academia emerged to build better hardware to support it. In this talk, we will discuss several issues regarding AI and hardware design. We will try to answer the following questions. Why do we need specialized hardware for AI? What does it mean "better" hardware for AI? Can AI support hardware beside the traditional computer-aided-design tools? How will AI evolve and how will this affect the hardware? During the talk, we will raise questions as much as we give answers.

 #machinelearning #ai #artificialintelligence #deeplearning

deltas.product_description: Webinars exploring hardware support for artificial intelligence

actual.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

Եթե ուզում եք ծրագրավորում սովորել, կարող եք դիմել rAcademy՝ հերթական դասընթացին մասնակցելու համար։

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

actual.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

#sastic

actual.review_text:
Եթե ուզում եք ծրագրավորում սովորել, կարող եք դիմել rAcademy՝ հերթական դասընթացին մասնակցելու համար։

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

Առաջին փուլը իր մեջ է ներառում երեք դասընթացներ՝

- Digital thinking
- Discrete Math
- Software development essentials with Java

Երկրորդ փուլի ընթացքում կուսումնասիրենք հետևյալ առարկաները՝

- Data Structures
- Algorithms
- Data Engineering

Դասընթացները տևելու են 3-4 ամիս՝ շաբաթական 3 անգամյա օնլայն հանդիպումներով ։ Դասավանդողների կողմից կիրականացվի հարցազրույց՝ դասընթացներում ընդգրկվելու համար։

Կա սիմվոլիկ վճար, որը ամբողջությամբ վերադարձվելու է լավ առաջադիմություն ցուցաբերած ուսանողներին։

Բոլոր մանրամասները և դիմելու կարգը տեսեք ֆեյսբուքի այս էջում․

deltas.product_description: Programming education and course offerings

actual.review_text:
Սիրելի հայրենակիցներ,

եթե դեռ ծանոթ չեք SASTIC նախաձեռնությանը և դրա նպատակներին, կարող եք շատ կարճ ժամանակում ծանոթանալ այստեղ․ 

Յուրաքանչյուր անհատի օգնությունը շատ կարևոր է այս գործին ավելի մեծ ընթացք տալու և ծրագրերն ավելի էֆեկտիվ իրագործելու համար։ Կարող եք օժանդակել թե ֆինանսապես, և թե ձեր մասնագիտական հմտություններով։

Եթե կասկածներ ունեք ծրագրերի, կամ ընդհանրապես նախաձեռնության վերաբերյալ, SASTIC-ի անդամները մեծ հաճույքով կպատասխանեն ձեր հարցերին․ ։

Հուսով եմ՝ պատերազմի օրերին արտահայտված համախմբվածությունը և հայրենիքի զարգացմանն ուղղված գործունեությունը գոնե որոշ չափով կարտահայտվի նաև այստեղ։

Շնորհակալություն ուշադրության համար

#sastic

prediction.review_text:
As we delve into the intersection of programming theory and practical application, it's crucial to emphasize the foundational role that hands-on experience plays in mastering our craft. While theory provides the groundwork, it is through real-world practice that we truly innovate and grasp the complexities of our field.

In my journey as a software engineer at Microsoft, I've found that applying concepts such as binary numbers and control flow in actual projects not only solidifies my understanding but also enhances my problem-solving skills. This practical application is vital as we navigate the evolving landscape of technology.

I encourage everyone to engage in small projects or contribute to open-source initiatives that allow you to apply what you’ve learned. This approach bridges the gap between theory and practice, deepening your comprehension of the technologies we utilize daily and promoting accessibility in our field.

Let’s continue to push the boundaries of what we can achieve together, leveraging our skills to create a meaningful impact in our communities and industries. If you’re seeking resources or mentorship, please reach out. Together, we can foster a supportive environment that emphasizes resilience, adaptability, and shared learning experiences.

actual.review_text:
#programmingWithArsen

I know the first topics seem boring, so I decided to do some exercises for several reasons:

1. show that the "boring theory" is important
2. show that we still need more "boring theory" before we can do something exciting
3. make sure you understand how the things I tell about are used in the code

This week will be all about problem solving, or trying to solve at least.

deltas.product_description: Learning and applying programming theory through practice

🚀 I'm excited to share the launch of a new series dedicated to programming concepts and hands-on exercises! In our ever-evolving tech landscape, it's crucial to make programming accessible and engaging for everyone, regardless of their background. 

This series will cover essential programming topics such as binary numbers, functions, and control flow, with a strong focus on practical applications and the effective integration of technology in our coding practices. Each post will feature hands-on exercises that you can try out right away, reinforcing your understanding through real-world scenarios. 

Learning to code is more than just writing code; it's about enhancing problem-solving skills and critical thinking. Whether you're just starting or looking to sharpen your skills, I hope these resources will empower you on your programming journey. 

Let’s build a supportive community where we can learn together, share our experiences, and uplift one another. I encourage you to reach out with questions or share your progress! Together, we can explore the transformative impacts of technology in our lives while effectively using these tools to foster growth and innovation. 💻✨ #ProgrammingWithArsen #DevOps #CloudSecurity #CommunitySupport #LearningTogether #EducationAccessibility

actual.review_text:
#programmingWithArsen 

Last of the three problems : finding the square root of an integer.

Next week we'll continue with more basic language constructs and their syntax. After 2nd chapter is over, we'll have some more tools handy to write something useful, and will get into less "boring" stuff.

actual.review_text:
Attention all friends and fellow engineers! I'm reaching out to you today because I'm in dire need of your help. Unfortunately, some truly exceptional engineers were let go from our team this week. These colleagues have been instrumental in my growth and development within the company, and I've learned so much from them

That's why I'm reaching out to you, my trusted network. I was hoping that perhaps someone out there might be able to offer a referral for these amazing engineers. They are not only incredibly talented, but also wonderful individuals to work with. They have a passion for their craft that is unmatched, and their expertise has made a significant impact on our team.

So, if you or someone you know is looking to hire talented and driven engineers, please don't hesitate to reach out. I would be forever grateful for your help in finding a new home for these amazing individuals. Together, we can help them land on their feet and continue to thrive in their careers. Thank you in advance for your help!

Some additional info:
- These engineers are based in Vancouver or in the US
- Focused more on backend development though have full stack experience

Edit: Thanks for all the help everyone! Please keep them coming!

deltas.product_description: Referrals and support for talented engineers seeking new opportunities

prediction.review_text:
Navigating the challenges of AI implementations in enterprise settings is a critical topic that deserves our attention. As we explore the potential of AI, we must confront the common hurdles that can impede successful deployment. Key challenges include data silos, integration issues, and the need for effective tool utilization. It's essential to align AI initiatives with business objectives, ensuring that technology adoption serves a clear purpose and drives measurable outcomes.

Collaboration between technical teams and business stakeholders is vital to bridge these gaps and create impactful solutions. Moreover, fostering a culture of continuous learning and adaptation is crucial. AI implementation is not a one-time effort; it requires ongoing refinement based on real-world feedback and insights.

Mentorship and community support play a significant role in empowering teams to navigate these challenges effectively. By sharing our experiences and insights, we can collectively unlock the transformative potential of AI in our industries. What challenges have you encountered in your AI implementations, and how have you addressed them? Let's engage in this dialogue and support each other on this journey.

actual.review_text:
Morgan nails this description of the kind of problems enterprises are facing TODAY trying to wrangle AI usage internally. We're working to fix that within the AI Gateway Working Group, and we'd love your feedback! 

Check out our repo to find our current proposals, meeting times, and charter:

deltas.product_description: Challenges in enterprise AI implementations

prediction.review_text:
As we delve deeper into the integration of AI within our networking capabilities, it's essential to focus on the practical barriers to entry that organizations encounter. The complexity of service networking can often hinder teams from fully utilizing powerful tools like Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and Istio.

In my experience, simplifying these processes is crucial for democratizing access to technology. We should prioritize creating clear documentation, offering hands-on tutorials, and fostering a community that promotes knowledge sharing. This approach not only empowers teams to adopt these technologies but also enhances collaboration across various sectors.

I invite everyone to share their insights on overcoming these integration challenges. What strategies have you found effective in making service networking more accessible? Together, let's build a more inclusive tech landscape where everyone can leverage the power of AI and cloud-native solutions.

actual.review_text:
So excited to see this launch! 

Lowering the barrier to entry for adopting powerful service networking capabilities and modern Gateway API configuration is a huge enabler for helping users benefit from all the innovative work that has been happening in the open source projects at core of this product!

deltas.product_description: Barriers to entry and service networking capabilities

prediction.review_text:
As we navigate the rapidly evolving landscape of AI and platform engineering, it’s essential to focus on the pressing challenges we face in effectively integrating these technologies. A significant hurdle is ensuring our AI implementations are not only technically sound but also accessible and beneficial to a diverse range of users. 

From my experience, leveraging scenario-based strategies that emphasize practical applications of AI tools is key. It’s crucial to go beyond the technical intricacies and highlight how these solutions can democratize access to technology, especially in critical sectors like education and healthcare.

Moreover, fostering a culture of collaboration and mentorship within our community is vital for enhancing our collective resilience. By sharing our experiences—both successes and setbacks—we empower each other to adapt and thrive in this fast-paced environment.

Let’s continue to push the boundaries of what’s possible with AI while prioritizing inclusivity and real-world impact. I encourage everyone to share their thoughts and experiences on this topic. What challenges have you encountered in AI integration, and how have you overcome them?

actual.review_text:
I sat down with The AI Kubernetes Show at KubeCon Atlanta 2025 to discuss the biggest challenges AI is bringing to our platforms.

In this limited podcast series by @Buoyant, I chatted with @William Chia about the challenges of bridging the divide between platform engineering and data science. Listen to the full episode now, and let me know what you think in the comments section. 
 

Be sure to check out Chris Khanoyan and Ahmed Bebars's episode, which went live today as well. And look forward to Dinesh Majrekar, Grant Miller, and Ramiro Berrelleza podcasts coming up next week. 

#KubeCon #Kubernetes #AI #PlatformEngineering #KubernetesAIShow

deltas.product_description: Challenges and discussions in AI and platform engineering

prediction.review_text:
Reflecting on my journey in the tech industry, I am continually reminded of the profound impact that mentorship and shared experiences have had on my career development. From my time at Microsoft to founding Avocadocore, I've learned that the most valuable lessons often come from those who have walked the path before us. 

Mentorship is not just about guidance; it's about building a community where we can learn from each other's successes and challenges. I've been fortunate to have mentors who have invested their time and wisdom in me, and I strive to pay it forward by supporting others in their career transitions. 

Navigating the complexities of backend development, exploring the exciting world of AI, or transitioning into a new role can be daunting. It's essential to remember that you are not alone in these challenges. Mental health and resilience play a crucial role in our journeys, and fostering a supportive community can make all the difference. 

Let's continue to cultivate a culture of collaboration and support in our tech community. Share your experiences, seek guidance, and don’t hesitate to reach out for help. Together, we can empower each other to achieve our goals and drive innovation in our fields. 

What lessons have you learned from your mentors? I’d love to hear your stories in the comments!

actual.review_text:
I worked under Mike when he was a team lead at IConect. I learned so much from him about what it takes to build awesome customer experiences through software.

Highly recommend any work place where you get to work with engineers like Mike!

I'm happy to present to open-source enthusiasts and computer science students about the intricacies of OpenTelemetry and how it can be leveraged to build efficient and scalable systems.

I'll also showcase how telemetry data can be fed into a data visualization tool like Grafana Labs' Grafana with the help of the `grafana-lgtm` docker container stack.

Thank you for this opportunity, Răzvan Deaconescu and ROSEdu.
Thank you to everyone who contributes to the open-source space.

prediction.review_text:
As a software engineer at Microsoft, I've experienced the profound importance of caregiver leave in creating a truly supportive workplace. This policy is not merely a checkbox for diversity and inclusion; it is essential for employee well-being and productivity. When team members feel supported in their personal lives, they can bring their best selves to work. 

During my recent caregiver leave, I was reminded of how a flexible and understanding workplace can significantly impact mental health and resilience. It allowed me to focus on my family, knowing my team was there to support me. 

I urge leaders in tech to champion robust caregiver leave policies. By prioritizing the emotional and psychological well-being of our teams alongside their technical skills, we can cultivate an environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive both personally and professionally. Together, let’s build a culture that embraces diversity and supports our colleagues in their personal journeys. 

#DiversityAndInclusion #CaregiverLeave #MentalHealth #SupportiveWorkplace #Microsoft

actual.review_text: Cannot agree more, the last two weeks of caregiver leave I received was super helpful for me to take care of my father. Over and above the immense support from my manager & LT during this time ensured that despite this being unplanned I did not receive a single call/ping from business & my team! I was able to focus on my father's health only. #microsoftlife #microsoftculture

prediction.review_text:
As I reflect on my career journey, I am filled with gratitude for the transitions that have shaped my path. Each role, from Adobe to Amazon and now at Microsoft, has not only honed my technical skills but also deepened my understanding of the importance of community and mentorship in our industry. 

Navigating transitions can often feel daunting, but I’ve learned that embracing change is a powerful opportunity for growth and resilience. It’s crucial to recognize that these experiences can teach us invaluable lessons about adaptability and the emotional aspects of our careers. 

I encourage everyone facing their own career transitions to seek support from your network. Share your experiences, ask questions, and lean on mentors who can guide you through challenges. Remember, it’s not just about the technical skills we acquire, but also the relationships we build along the way. 

Let’s continue to foster a culture of inclusivity and support within our tech community. Together, we can empower each other to overcome impostor syndrome and reach new heights. What has your career transition taught you? I’d love to hear your stories!

actual.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe

deltas.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

actual.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?

Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.

actual.review_text:
Found this LinkedIn Insight about "career athleticism" and looked at my own path.
The first thing I'm struggling with is: where does the investment in one's learning lie? At many points in my career I noticed that I wanted to do more, learned in my private time, got more pay, but the skills I got weren't needed, so I had to change my workplace to apply the new skillset. I would've preferred to just stick to the same workplace and develop myself there. There's an old expression that says something along the lines of: "Everyone has knowledge someone else paid for." The portfolio approach makes an assumption that all skills can be acquired outside of a job, which is false. Also if a higher investment in building the portfolio is made, it will be factored into the asked salary. Another thing means that the gig itself would make its way into the employee's portfolio, would any company accept these details to be public?
The second challenge is building teams. Working in software development with gig programmers sounds a lot like consultancy, and most of the time the code quality and maintainability suffers.
Anyway that's my view on the data based candidate search.

actual.review_text:
All great things come to an end!!

Today is my last working day with Adobe!! Spent wonderful 5+ years with this great company.

Coming from a small village and being completed most of my education in government schools/colleges(Marathi medium), I never thought of working for a company like Adobe (and previous company Amazon).

Got to learn so much both personally and professionally, spent some of the best time of my life, made some of the best friends and got inspired by some of the best brains in the industry which will be there for life!

With that I want to say goodbye to this wonderful journey with Adobe! Hope our path crosses in the future!!

Excited for the journey ahead!!

#Gratitude #Adobelife #Adobe

deltas.product_description: Reflection on career journey and transitions in employment

Navigating the job market in tech can indeed feel overwhelming, especially when breaking into a new role or industry. Reflecting on my own journey, I recognize the vital role of resilience and adaptability during career transitions. It's essential to approach job searching not merely as a task but as an opportunity for personal growth and community engagement.

1. **Leverage Your Network**: Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, and industry connections. They can offer insights, referrals, and sometimes even opportunities that aren't publicly advertised. Building these connections fosters community support.

2. **Upskill and Reskill**: The tech landscape is ever-evolving. Investing time in learning new skills, particularly in AI and cloud technologies, can enhance your attractiveness as a candidate. Platforms like Microsoft Learn provide excellent resources to help you on this journey.

3. **Showcase Your Projects**: Use platforms like GitHub or personal blogs to demonstrate your technical skills through real-world applications. This not only highlights your expertise but also showcases your passion for the field, encouraging others in the community to share their work too.

4. **Stay Resilient**: Rejections are part of the process. Each 'no' brings you closer to a 'yes.' Surround yourself with a supportive community that motivates you to keep pushing forward, emphasizing the importance of mental health during these transitions.

5. **Seek Mentorship**: Engaging with mentors can provide invaluable guidance and perspective. They can help you navigate the complexities of job searching and share their experiences, fostering a culture of learning and support.

Remember, the journey is just as important as the destination. Embrace the learning opportunities that come your way, and don't hesitate to share your experiences with others. Together, we can build a stronger, more supportive tech community. #CareerDevelopment #JobSearching #TechCommunity #Resilience #Mentorship

actual.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar

deltas.product_description: Career experiences in the video game industry

prediction.review_text:
As someone who has navigated the complexities of higher education in the U.S., I want to share some insights for those considering this path, especially international students facing unique challenges. Pursuing a degree here can be a transformative experience, but it comes with hurdles that require careful navigation. 

First, thorough research is crucial. Understand the programs that align with your career goals and the institutions that offer them. Don’t hesitate to reach out to current students or alumni; their firsthand experiences can provide invaluable perspectives and help you gauge the quality of online education, which can sometimes fall short of expectations.

Second, consider the financial implications. Look into scholarships, grants, and assistantships that can ease the burden. Many universities offer resources to help international students find funding opportunities, which are essential given the financial strain that can accompany studying abroad.

Lastly, embrace the community. Engage with peers, join student organizations, and attend networking events. Building connections can open doors and provide support throughout your academic journey. Remember, it’s not just about the degree; it’s about the relationships and experiences you cultivate along the way, especially as you navigate visa restrictions that can impact job prospects post-graduation.

If anyone has questions or needs guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #EducationChallenges

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

deltas.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

None of them seem to be able to mark a customer as "not interested" and not call again. Really disappointed with you. I will also complain to banking ombudsman and to Dept of Telecom (DOT) for misusing our contact number to spam us with marketing calls. Shameful

deltas.product_description: Customer experience with financial services and marketing practices

actual.review_text:
This post is dedicated to my past employer, Ubisoft, and to my time spent working as a QA/QC engineer for the `Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora` video game.

I am thrilled to witness the release of the game and the positive reviews that have come along. Avatar is a vast franchise, spanning many fields of creativity.

A big thanks goes to Ubisoft Romania (Bucharest studio) for having me work on such a masterpiece game. I want to extend this appreciation to my manager at that time, Andrei-Claudiu Maracineanu-Manolescu for believing in me since the first day. You have my sincere appreciation.

My tenure at Ubisoft was essential for my career growth.
Thank you for having me be part of the journey.

P.S: If you've completed the game, you can find my name in the credits section dedicated to the Bucharest Ubisoft studio.

#Ubisoft #Avatar

deltas.product_description: Career experiences in the video game industry

prediction.review_text:
As someone who has navigated the complexities of higher education in the U.S., I want to share some insights for those considering this path, especially international students who face unique challenges. Pursuing a degree here can be a transformative experience, but it comes with hurdles that require careful navigation.

First, thorough research is crucial. Understand the programs that align with your career goals and the institutions that offer them. Don’t hesitate to reach out to current students or alumni; their firsthand experiences can provide invaluable perspectives and help you gauge the quality of online education, which can sometimes fall short of expectations.

Second, consider the financial implications. Look into scholarships, grants, and assistantships that can ease the burden. Many universities offer resources to help international students find funding opportunities, which are essential given the financial strain that can accompany studying abroad.

Lastly, embrace the community. Engage with peers, join student organizations, and attend networking events. Building connections can open doors and provide support throughout your academic journey. Remember, it’s not just about the degree; it’s about the relationships and experiences you cultivate along the way, especially as you navigate visa restrictions that can impact job prospects post-graduation.

If anyone has questions or needs guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other in this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #EducationChallenges

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

deltas.product_description: Advice on pursuing higher education in the United States

prediction.review_text:
Navigating the job market in tech can feel overwhelming, especially when breaking into a new role or industry. Reflecting on my own journey, I recognize the importance of viewing job searching as an opportunity for personal growth and community engagement. Here are some actionable strategies that have been beneficial for me and may assist you as well:

1. **Leverage Your Network**: Reach out to former colleagues, mentors, and industry connections. They can offer insights, referrals, and sometimes even opportunities that aren't publicly advertised. Building these connections fosters community support and emphasizes the importance of mentorship.

2. **Upskill and Reskill**: The tech landscape is ever-evolving. Investing time in learning new skills, particularly in AI and cloud technologies, can enhance your attractiveness as a candidate. Platforms like Microsoft Learn provide excellent resources to help you on this journey.

3. **Showcase Your Projects**: Use platforms like GitHub or personal blogs to demonstrate your technical skills through real-world applications. This not only highlights your expertise but also showcases your passion for the field, encouraging others in the community to share their work too.

4. **Stay Resilient**: Rejections are part of the process. Each 'no' brings you closer to a 'yes.' Surround yourself with a supportive community that motivates you to keep pushing forward, emphasizing the importance of mental health during these transitions.

5. **Seek Mentorship**: Engaging with mentors can provide invaluable guidance and perspective. They can help you navigate the complexities of job searching and share their experiences, fostering a culture of learning and support.

Remember, the journey is just as important as the destination. Embrace the learning opportunities that come your way, and don't hesitate to share your experiences with others. Together, we can build a stronger, more supportive tech community. #CareerDevelopment #JobSearching #TechCommunity #Resilience #Mentorship

prediction.review_text:
Navigating higher education in the U.S. can be a transformative yet challenging journey, especially for international students. Here are some insights based on my experiences that may help you along the way:

1. **Thorough Research**: It's essential to understand which programs align with your career goals and the institutions that offer them. Don’t hesitate to connect with current students or alumni; their firsthand experiences can provide invaluable insights into the quality of education and the realities of online learning, which can sometimes fall short of expectations.

2. **Financial Considerations**: Investigate scholarships, grants, and assistantships that can alleviate financial burdens. Many universities have resources dedicated to helping international students find funding opportunities, which are crucial given the financial strain that often accompanies studying abroad.

3. **Community Engagement**: Embrace the community by engaging with peers, joining student organizations, and attending networking events. Building connections is vital; it not only enriches your academic experience but also provides support as you navigate visa restrictions that may impact job prospects post-graduation.

4. **Mental Well-being**: Remember that pursuing a degree is not just about academic qualifications; it’s also about taking care of your mental health. Embrace the challenges as learning opportunities and seek support when needed. Resilience is key in this journey.

5. **Quality of Education**: Be mindful of the quality of education you are pursuing. Research the reputation of programs and their alignment with industry standards. This can significantly impact your career trajectory and job prospects after graduation.

If you have questions or need guidance, feel free to reach out. Let’s support each other on this journey! #HigherEducation #InternationalStudents #Mentorship #CommunitySupport #EducationChallenges #MentalHealth #Resilience #Productivity

actual.review_text:
As an international student who came to the US in 2008 and who benefited from the quality of education here, I have an advice for those who were thinking of coming to US for higher education any time soon (this year and 2021) - DO NOT come to the US for your higher education. There are 2 reasons for this that are straightforward:

(1) Quality of education on any online tool is subpar as compared to the on campus experience. Most classes are now being conducted online. I will be honest - it’s not worth paying the kind of money international students pay in tuition. There is no question that US has some of the best faculty but still the quality of online education would make you question what you are paying for. Several US higher education institutions today have impressive buildings and campuses, in many cases funded by higher tuition charged to international students. But, if you are not able to get any benefit from it, then what’s the point. 

(2) Several companies in US are not willing to hire international students with visa requirements. It’s likely there will be more visa restrictions imposed in this economy which will also limit your ability to find an internship or job. If you are paying upwards of 50000$ for a degree, you need to have a plan to pay that massive loan.

actual.review_text:
If you are interested in Data Engineering and you are based on LATAM, please read this!

At Factored we are on a mission to make a positive contribution to LATAM. The way we want to do this is by building high-caliber data teams ready to show the world the talent we have in Latin America.

Data: 16 de Outubro, às 19h BRT. 

[English]
World statistics estimate that "around 70% of people in the world will experience Imposter Syndrome at least once in their lives"( It is likely to affect women.

Join us to know more about Imposter Syndrome and hear the amazing stories from talented professionals that overcame it.
Date: October 16th at 7PM BRT (GMT-3) 
Note: the event will be held in Portuguese.

As we delve into the integration of AI within enterprise systems, I want to share insights specifically on how SAP S/4HANA can leverage AI functionalities to enhance financial processes. One key area is the automation of COGS splits, which can significantly reduce manual errors and improve reporting accuracy. By utilizing machine learning algorithms, we can predict variances in production costs and automate the reconciliation process, making our workflows more efficient. 

I encourage my fellow SAP practitioners to consider how AI can streamline their specific workflows. For those interested, I've compiled a detailed step-by-step guide on setting up these functionalities in your test systems, ensuring accessibility and practical application. Let’s embrace these technologies to not only improve our processes but also empower our teams with the tools they need for success. 

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need further resources on this topic. Together, we can drive innovation in our finance practices while supporting each other in our learning journeys!

actual.review_text:
Good Day Friends, 
I would like to share my understanding of "COGS Split" in SAP S/4 HANA 1809 in a quick presentation.

This concept has been since 'SFin 1.0' as its one of much requested features from SAP customers.

Refer Note 2399030 (Splitting the costs of goods sold in S/4HANA)

Hope you will find it useful to some extent. Thank you. 

 #sapfico #hana #COGSSplit #S/4HANAFinance1809 #sap

🚀 I'm thrilled to share insights on building web applications using modern development frameworks! As we embrace the cloud and AI, selecting the right tools is essential for enhancing productivity and fostering collaboration within our teams. 

Frameworks like React and Django are transformative, offering features that streamline development while ensuring scalability and security. Here are some key takeaways: 

1️⃣ **Choose the Right Framework**: Assess your project needs. React is excellent for dynamic user interfaces, while Django is perfect for rapid development with a strong focus on security. 

2️⃣ **Leverage Cloud Services**: Integrating Azure or AWS can significantly boost your app's performance and reliability. Consider exploring serverless architectures for cost efficiency and scalability! 

3️⃣ **Prioritize User Experience**: Always keep the end-user in mind. Utilize tools that facilitate easy testing and feedback loops to iterate quickly and effectively. 

4️⃣ **Stay Updated**: The tech landscape evolves rapidly. Regularly update your skills and knowledge about new features and best practices in your chosen framework. 

5️⃣ **Engage with the Community**: Share your experiences and learn from others. Platforms like GitHub and Stack Overflow are invaluable for troubleshooting and gaining new perspectives. 

Building web applications is not just about coding; it's about creating solutions that have a real impact. Let’s continue to support one another in our journeys and share our learnings! 💡💪 #WebDevelopment #React #Django #CloudComputing #AI #CommunitySupport

actual.review_text:
Just finished the course “Learning Django”!

Implemented models, enabled migrations, built URL handlers and URL patterns, views and templates to create a Django web application that manages Pet Adoption.

Also touched on essential practices like regular expressions and incorporating CSS and JS into webapp.

Big shout out to course author: Caleb Smith 

#django
#webdevelopment
#linkedinlearning

deltas.product_description: Building web applications using development frameworks

prediction.review_text:
🚀 Excited to share some insights on SAP S/4HANA system activation and the foundational processes that can empower your enterprise finance operations! 

Activating your SAP S/4HANA system is a crucial first step in leveraging its capabilities for enhanced financial management and reporting. Here’s a quick guide to get you started:

1️⃣ **System Activation**: Ensure you have access to SAP CAL (Cloud Appliance Library) to quickly set up your test environment. This allows you to experiment without impacting your production systems.

2️⃣ **Basic Process Understanding**: Familiarize yourself with key processes such as COGS splits and Account Based COPA. Understanding these concepts is essential for accurate financial reporting and analysis.

3️⃣ **Hands-On Learning**: I recommend utilizing resources like SAP notes and community blogs to deepen your understanding. Engage with tutorials on platforms like SlideShare and YouTube for step-by-step guidance.

4️⃣ **Community Engagement**: Don’t hesitate to reach out for mentorship or support. Sharing experiences and challenges can significantly enhance your learning journey. 

Let’s democratize access to SAP knowledge and empower each other in our professional growth! If you have any questions or need further resources, feel free to connect. Together, we can navigate the complexities of SAP S/4HANA and explore its transformative impacts on finance operations! 💡 #SAP #S4HANA #Finance #CommunitySupport #LearningTogether

actual.review_text:
Are you new to #SAP #S4HANA?

I strongly recommend you do the below:

1) Activate the S4HANA 1809 free test system from SAP CAL. (I've published a video on how to do this on my YouTube channel)

2) Follow this very simple PDF to run some basic processes in Finance, Procurement, and Sales.

This will help you get a general understanding of how the system works

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

deltas.product_description: SAP S4HANA system activation and basic process understanding

As technology evolves, the integration of brand marketing strategies with tech solutions is vital for businesses aiming to thrive in a competitive landscape. At Microsoft, I've witnessed how leveraging cloud solutions, AI, and automation can revolutionize marketing efforts and enhance customer engagement. 2. **Personalization through AI**: Implement AI-driven tools to create personalized experiences for customers, improving engagement and fostering brand loyalty.
3. **Seamless Integration**: Ensure that your marketing platforms integrate smoothly with your existing tech stack, reducing friction and allowing for a cohesive strategy.
4. **Community Engagement**: Build a community around your brand by encouraging feedback and interaction through social media and other channels. This enhances trust and strengthens your brand’s reputation.

NIKE SALUTES CHANDRAYAN

Activating your SAP S/4HANA system is a crucial first step to leverage its capabilities for improved financial management and reporting. Here’s a straightforward guide to get you started:

actual.review_text:
Are you new to #SAP #S4HANA?

I strongly recommend you do the below:

1) Activate the S4HANA 1809 free test system from SAP CAL. (I've published a video on how to do this on my YouTube channel)

2) Follow this very simple PDF to run some basic processes in Finance, Procurement, and Sales.

This will help you get a general understanding of how the system works

As you see this is a public PDF created by SAP, so please feel free to download and share it.

#AGALAL #Share_Knowledge

prediction.review_text:
As we delve into the transformative potential of AI in healthcare, I'm increasingly inspired by its profound impact on diagnostics and research. The integration of AI technologies enables us to analyze vast datasets, leading to faster and more accurate diagnoses, which ultimately enhances patient outcomes and streamlines research processes. This capability allows us to uncover insights that were previously unattainable.

At Microsoft, we are dedicated to developing solutions that empower healthcare professionals with the necessary tools to effectively leverage AI. By concentrating on practical applications, such as predictive analytics and machine learning models, we can tackle real-world challenges in healthcare delivery.

I invite my fellow technology and healthcare professionals to collaborate and share insights on how we can further harness AI's capabilities. Together, we can drive innovation that democratizes access to quality healthcare, ensuring that advancements benefit everyone. Let's continue this vital conversation and support one another in our shared journey of discovery and improvement. #AI #Healthcare #Innovation #Collaboration #Microsoft

actual.review_text:
Not many of you may know but I spent a lot of my career in the pathology laboratories doing software engineering.

I have often thought how AI will revolutionize patient care, especially in terms of diagnostic services such as radiology and pathology. So it was exciting to see new research from my current employer Microsoft and my former, the UW, rolling research into exactly this, using AI to improve cancer outcomes.

Thinking of the good old days when Rodney Schmidt and I were trying "change the world" with software!

deltas.product_description: AI impact on healthcare diagnostics and research

prediction.review_text:
As we navigate the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence, it's essential to focus on practical applications and the collaborative efforts that drive innovation in our industry. 🌍🤝 

In my experience at Microsoft and through my previous roles, I've witnessed how global collaboration enhances the accessibility of AI advancements. When teams unite their expertise across borders, we not only improve AI capabilities but also ensure that these innovations benefit a broader audience, especially in areas like education and customer support redesign.

However, we must also recognize the competitive nature of AI development. While organizations strive to harness AI's potential to enhance their products and services, we must address the ethical implications and ensure inclusivity in our approaches.

As professionals in this space, we have a responsibility to advocate for a balanced strategy—one that promotes healthy competition while prioritizing community engagement and shared learning. Let's leverage our collective knowledge to create AI solutions that are not only powerful but also equitable and responsible.

I invite you all to share your thoughts on how we can integrate collaboration into our strategies for AI development. What initiatives have you found effective in bridging gaps and driving inclusive innovation? Let's learn from each other! #AI #Collaboration #Innovation #Ethics #CommunitySupport

actual.review_text:
“The battle for #artificialintelligence supremacy is a binary game between US & China ”— this is what the media likes you to believe. 

My last talk in Berlin debunked exactly this propagated myth. 

I had highlighted that AI innovation can and will come from anywhere since:
 1) talent, 
2)Infrastructure and 
3) AI tech & tools 
are accessible to anyone with internet and hunger to solve a problem. 

Do you agree?

deltas.product_description: Global competition and collaboration in artificial intelligence

These datasets not only speed up the development process but also democratize access to vital resources, fostering innovation across various sectors.

3. **Udacity Self-Driving Car Dataset**: A comprehensive collection for training models across diverse driving conditions.

Leveraging these datasets can significantly enhance your projects, whether you're creating a proof of concept or engaging in research. Remember, successful AI implementation hinges not only on technology but also on our commitment to responsible and inclusive practices. actual.review_text:
Wow , Lyft just open sourced its autonomous driving dataset from its Level 5 self-driving fleet!

Awesome stuff, Logan Green & team

actual.review_text: Azure Virtual Network Manager (AvNM) allows you to manage your networks at scale, but it's not just for the big companies. Anyone can benefit from the features provided. Give it a try and always feel free to share your thoughts with me and her.

deltas.product_description: Network management tools and accessibility

Thrilled to announce the launch of our latest feature in Azure: the Azure Virtual Network Manager! This tool is designed to tackle the challenges of effective cloud networking at scale, simplifying the management of routing and IP allocations while ensuring a security-first design. 🚀 By addressing key customer pain points in network management, we aim to enhance operational simplicity and empower teams to innovate. 

A huge thank you to my dedicated team for their hard work and commitment to excellence in bringing this feature to life. Your efforts are what drive our success. 

For those eager to explore further, I invite you to check out the comprehensive documentation and tutorials we've prepared. Let's work together to harness the full potential of Azure and transform our approach to cloud networking. Together, we can make technology more accessible and impactful! #Azure #CloudNetworking #TeamSuccess #Innovation #AIIntegration

actual.review_text: Excited to see Virtual Network Verifier (or VNV as we call it) get launched to public preview!! One of the biggest challenges for customers is being able to verify traffic flows within their complex configurations. With VNV customers can now very quickly answer questions like "Why can't my VM reach my database?" Please take a moment to go try it for yourself.

deltas.product_description: Technology product launch and customer engagement

prediction.review_text:
Excited to share insights on the evolving routing management capabilities in cloud environments! 🌐 As we continue to push the boundaries of what's possible with Azure, the importance of efficient and secure routing cannot be overstated. 

In today's cloud-first world, effective routing management is crucial for ensuring seamless connectivity and performance. With recent advancements in Azure, we can leverage features like User Defined Routes (UDR) and Network Security Groups (NSG) to enhance our routing strategies. These tools simplify network traffic management and bolster our security posture, allowing us to address customer pain points more effectively.

I encourage my fellow engineers and tech enthusiasts to dive into the documentation and explore how these capabilities can be integrated into your projects. Let's share our experiences and learn from one another as we navigate this exciting landscape together! 

A big shout-out to my team for their hard work in making these enhancements a reality. Your dedication to operational simplicity and security-first design truly makes a difference! 💪 #Azure #CloudNetworking #RoutingManagement #Teamwork

actual.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

deltas.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

In today's cloud-first world, effective routing management ensures seamless connectivity and performance. With recent advancements in Azure, we can leverage features like User Defined Routes (UDR) and Network Security Groups (NSG) to enhance our routing strategies. These tools not only simplify network traffic management but also strengthen our security posture, allowing us to address customer pain points more effectively.

It's essential for businesses to integrate these capabilities into their operations to optimize performance and security. I encourage my fellow engineers and tech enthusiasts to dive into the documentation and explore how these features can be integrated into your projects. Let's navigate this exciting landscape together, sharing practical applications and solutions along the way! 

A big shout-out to my team for their hard work in making these enhancements a reality. Your dedication to operational simplicity and security-first design truly makes a difference! 💪 #Azure #CloudNetworking #RoutingManagement #PracticalSolutions #TeamAchievements

actual.review_text:
Today is a great day for managing your routing at scale in Azure! I'm happy to announce that my team is delivering the capability to manage your user-defined route tables at scale.

One of the complexities customers face with route tables in Azure is the management of linking them to multiple subnets and then managing the routing rules at a larger scale. With route management functionality coming into Azure Virtual Network Manager, customers can now simply create a single definition and apply it to hundreds of subnets with just a few clicks.

Additionally customers have asked for the capability to have more than the 400 rule limit that exists today. Customers who use AVNM for their route management will be able to create up to 1,000 rules in their route tables. This gives our customers the ability to create more complex and detailed route tables.

Great work by my team and I look forward to hearing how this feature helps your organization.

deltas.product_description: Routing management capabilities in cloud environments

prediction.review_text:
Thrilled to announce the launch of the Azure Virtual Network Manager! This feature is crafted to directly address the specific challenges of routing management and IP allocations in cloud networking, all while maintaining a security-first approach. 🚀 By honing in on key customer pain points, we aim to simplify operations and empower teams to innovate confidently.

A heartfelt thank you to my incredible team for their dedication and hard work in bringing this feature to life. Your contributions are essential to our success.

For those looking to dive deeper, I encourage you to explore the comprehensive documentation and tutorials we've developed. Together, we can harness the full potential of Azure, making technology more accessible and impactful for all! #Azure #CloudNetworking #CustomerEngagement #Innovation #AIIntegration

actual.review_text: Excited to see Virtual Network Verifier (or VNV as we call it) get launched to public preview!! One of the biggest challenges for customers is being able to verify traffic flows within their complex configurations. With VNV customers can now very quickly answer questions like "Why can't my VM reach my database?" Please take a moment to go try it for yourself.

deltas.product_description: Technology product launch and customer engagement
